title: mentha aquatica subsp. verticillata
intro: Mentha aquatica subsp. verticillata, also known as water mint or peppermint, is a subspecies of mint plant that thrives in wet environments and is characterized by its bright green leaves and distinctive fragrance. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its essential oils, which contain menthol and menthone, known for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica subsp. verticillata include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and improve mental clarity. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make topical creams, inhalers, and mouthwashes, among other applications.
